template_name: Standard Prescription Format – Circumcision in Neonates

timing.ideal_timing: After 48 hours of age, once infant is stable, feeding well, and has passed urine

procedure_options.name: Traditional Surgical Circumcision
procedure_options.notes: Commonly used in hospital or clinic settings

procedure_options.name: Plastibell Device
procedure_options.notes: Ring usually falls off in 5–10 days, less bleeding

procedure_options.name: Mogen Clamp
procedure_options.notes: Fast, minimal bleeding, but risk of glans injury

counseling_points: Mild redness, swelling, or yellowish film may be normal
If Plastibell is used, ring usually falls off in 5–10 days
Explain warning signs of bleeding, infection, or urinary retention

benefits: Slightly lower risk of urinary tract infection in infancy
Reduced risk of phimosis and balanitis
Possible long-term reduction in some sexually transmitted infections

local_care: Apply PETROLEUM JELLY to prevent sticking to diaper

analgesia.note: Dose to be calculated as per weight

parent_advice: Mild redness, swelling, and yellow film can be part of normal healing
Bleeding should stop with gentle pressure
Seek urgent review if baby does not pass urine, develops fever, or has continuous bleeding
Do not apply home remedies, powders, or antiseptics unless advised
Follow-up if healing appears delayed or abnormal

follow_up.first_review: Within 24–72 hours or earlier if concern
follow_up.second_review: At 5–10 days if Plastibell used or as clinically indicated

opd_checklist: Stable term baby, feeding and voiding well?
Any contraindication such as hypospadias or chordee?
Parents educated about post-procedure warning signs?
counselling: Mild redness, swelling, and yellow film can be part of normal healing — हल्की लालिमा, सूजन और पीली परत सामान्य ठीक होने का हिस्सा है
Bleeding should stop with gentle pressure — हल्का दबाव देने से खून रुक जाना चाहिए
Seek urgent review if baby does not pass urine, develops fever, or has continuous bleeding — अगर बच्चा पेशाब न करे, बुखार हो, या लगातार खून बहे तो तुरंत डॉक्टर को दिखाएं
Do not apply home remedies, powders, or antiseptics unless advised — बिना डॉक्टर की सलाह के घरेलू उपचार, पाउडर या एंटीसेप्टिक न लगाएं
Apply petroleum jelly to prevent sticking to diaper — डायपर से चिपकने से बचाने के लिए वैसलीन लगाएं
Follow-up if healing appears delayed or abnormal — अगर ठीक होने में देरी लगे या असामान्य लगे तो दोबारा दिखाएं
warning_signs: Continuous bleeding that does not stop with pressure — लगातार खून बहना जो दबाव से न रुके
No urine output for more than 6-8 hours — 6-8 घंटे से ज़्यादा पेशाब न आना
Pus or foul-smelling discharge — मवाद या बदबूदार रिसाव
Excessive swelling or redness — बहुत ज़्यादा सूजन या लालिमा
Baby refusing feeds or becoming lethargic — बच्चे का दूध न पीना या सुस्त हो जाना
expected_course: Normal healing after circumcision takes 7-10 days. A yellow-white film (fibrin) over the wound is normal and should not be removed. Mild swelling resolves within 3-5 days. If Plastibell is used, the ring falls off in 5-10 days. Complete healing may take 2-3 weeks.
key_clinical_points: Absolute contraindications: hypospadias, epispadias, chordee, buried penis
Delay circumcision in preterm, LBW (<2.5 kg), sick, or bleeding disorder babies
Always check for hypospadias before circumcision — foreskin needed for repair
Plastibell technique: ring falls off in 5-10 days; less bleeding
Pain management: sucrose, EMLA cream, or dorsal penile nerve block
Yellow fibrin film is normal healing — not infection

severity_assessment.mild: Normal post-procedure appearance with mild swelling and yellow film — routine care with petroleum jelly
severity_assessment.moderate: Increased redness, delayed ring separation, mild oozing — close observation, possible antibiotics
severity_assessment.severe: Continuous bleeding, no urine output, pus, fever, glans injury — emergency evaluation, possible surgical intervention

monitoring_parameters.frequency: Every diaper change for first 24 hours
At first review (24-72 hours) and second review (5-10 days)
At each feed for first 24 hours
